auth_notes: Most modern MLS systems use the RESO Web API standard which requires OAuth2 (Client Credentials or Authorization Code). Older systems use RETS which requires Digest Authentication and custom headers (User-Agent-Password).
rate_limits: Highly variable by provider (e.g., Bridge Interactive, Trestle, or Spark); typically 1,000-5,000 calls per day.
gotchas: Data schemas (metadata) differ significantly between local boards even when following RESO standards.
RETS (legacy) returns XML/COMPACT format which is extremely difficult to parse in n8n without heavy JavaScript.
Media/Images often require secondary requests or have expiring signed URLs.
client_must_provide: Approved Data Access Level (IDX, VOW, or Back Office)
research_notes: Implementation requires the HTTP Request node using OData protocol. Users must manually handle pagination using '$skip' and '$top' parameters. Authentication should be handled via n8n's 'Generic OAuth2' credential type or a separate HTTP request to the token endpoint to retrieve a Bearer token.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Implementation requires the HTTP Request node using OData protocol. Users must manually handle pagination using '$skip' and '$top' parameters. Authentication should be handled via n8n's 'Generic OAuth2' credential type or a separate HTTP request to the token endpoint to retrieve a Bearer token.
